quiz.question:
In this position, Black offered a queen trade. 

The pawn structure is fixed, and we have a knight for Black's rook.

How should we react?

answers.text: We should avoid the trade since Black's rook is stronger than our knight. We'll go for a check on f7 and attack Black's kingside pawns to utilize our passed pawns.

feedback.text: Good job! The passed f-pawn is a big asset here, and having only the knight would be sufficient enough to win.

distractors.text: It is best to trade the queens. The passed f-pawn is too strong. Also, since every pawn on the chessboard is fixed, our knight is superior in this endgame.

feedback.text: Black can stop the f-pawn easily with ...Ra8. Also, the c3-pawn is weak.
